summary: redis-type-os impersonates redis-om and pulls an unused ulid look-alike transitive dependency
details: The package name, README, repository, and homepage all claim association with redis/redis-om-node (the legitimate Redis-published 'redis-om' library), and the bundled code is a copy of redis-om. The package.json declares a runtime dependency on 'os-ulid-void@^3.0.2', a name that closely resembles the legitimate 'ulid' package. The bundler configuration (tsup.noExternal includes 'ulid') inlines the real 'ulid' into dist/index.js, so 'os-ulid-void' is never actually required by the shipped code — yet installing this package still resolves and installs that dependency into the consumer's tree. This shape — publisher impersonation combined with an unused look-alike transitive — matches the namespace-abuse pattern where the shipped tarball is benign but the install action drags in another package whose contents are the actual payload. The contents of os-ulid-void itself were not assessed in this record.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
